name: azure-monitor-opentelemetry-py
description: One-line setup for Application Insights with OpenTelemetry auto-instrumentation. Use this skill to quickly configure Azure monitoring for Python applications with automatic telemetry collection for Flask, Django, FastAPI, and other popular frameworks.
capabilities: Generate one-line Azure Monitor setup code for Python applications
Provide configuration examples for Flask, Django, FastAPI, and other frameworks
Show how to add custom traces, metrics, and logs to Application Insights
Explain sampling configuration and cloud role name setup
Demonstrate Azure AD authentication for production workloads
List supported auto-instrumentation libraries and their telemetry types
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only documentation skill with no executable code. The static analyzer detected no suspicious patterns. The skill provides reference documentation for the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Python package - a legitimate Microsoft monitoring library. No security risks identified.
valueStatement: Setting up application monitoring in Azure requires understanding connection strings, auto-instrumentation, and telemetry configuration. This skill provides ready-to-use code snippets and best practices for adding Application Insights monitoring to Python applications with minimal code changes.
userTitle: Configure Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry for Python
limitations: This skill provides reference documentation only - it does not execute code or create resources in Azure
Requires a valid Azure Application Insights connection string to function
Does not cover on-premise or non-Azure monitoring solutions
Auto-instrumentation coverage depends on the libraries used in the target application

useCases.title: Add monitoring to a Flask web application
useCases.description: Quickly add Application Insights telemetry to an existing Flask API with auto-instrumentation for HTTP requests and exceptions

useCases.title: Set up production monitoring with AAD auth
useCases.description: Configure Azure Monitor with Azure Active Directory authentication instead of instrumentation keys for secure production deployments

useCases.title: Add custom business metrics to Application Insights
useCases.description: Create custom counters, metrics, and trace attributes to monitor business-specific events alongside auto-collected telemetry

promptTemplates.title: Basic Azure Monitor setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to add Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry to my Python application with just a few lines of code.
promptTemplates.scenario: Developer wants minimal setup for basic Application Insights monitoring

promptTemplates.title: Configure monitoring for Flask app
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I configure Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry for a Flask application? Show me the complete setup including imports and initialization.
promptTemplates.scenario: Flask developer needs specific framework integration

promptTemplates.title: Add custom traces and metrics
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to add custom traces with custom attributes and custom metrics to Application Insights using OpenTelemetry.
promptTemplates.scenario: Developer needs to track business-specific events

promptTemplates.title: Production configuration with AAD auth
promptTemplates.prompt: What is the best practice for configuring Azure Monitor in production using Azure AD authentication instead of connection strings?
promptTemplates.scenario: Security-conscious developer setting up production monitoring

bestPractices: Call configure_azure_monitor() before importing any instrumented libraries to ensure auto-instrumentation loads correctly
Use environment variables for connection strings in production instead of hardcoding them in source files
Set the cloud role name via the resource parameter to identify your service correctly in the Application Map
antiPatterns: Calling configure_azure_monitor() after importing instrumented libraries - this breaks auto-instrumentation
Hardcoding connection strings with instrumentation keys in source code - use environment variables instead
Enabling all instrumentations in low-memory environments - selectively enable only the frameworks you use

faq.question: What is Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry?
faq.answer: Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry is a Microsoft distribution of OpenTelemetry that sends telemetry data to Azure Application Insights. It provides auto-instrumentation for popular Python frameworks with minimal code changes.

faq.question: Do I need an Azure account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need an Azure subscription with an Application Insights resource. The skill helps you write the code, but you must create the Azure resource and obtain a connection string.

faq.question: What frameworks are automatically instrumented?
faq.answer: The distro automatically instruments Flask, Django, FastAPI, Starlette, Requests, urllib3, httpx, aiohttp, psycopg2, pymysql, pymongo, and redis. Full list is in the documentation.

faq.question: Can I use this without instrumentation keys?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use Azure AD authentication with DefaultAzureCredential instead of instrumentation keys. This is recommended for production environments.

faq.question: How do I filter or sample telemetry?
faq.answer: Use the sampling_ratio parameter in configure_azure_monitor() to set a value between 0.0 and 1.0. For example, sampling_ratio=0.1 sends 10% of telemetry.

faq.question: Does this work with serverless functions?
faq.answer: Yes, Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry works with Azure Functions. Add the configuration in your function app initialization code following the same patterns.
